title.en: TURNING; BORING (arrangements for copying or controlling B23Q)
Methods for turning or working essentially requiring the use of turning-machines; Use of auxiliary equipment in connection with such methods
General-purpose turning-machines or devices, e.g. centre lathes with feed rod and lead screw; Sets of turning-machines
Small lathes, e.g. for toolmakers (specially designed for watchmakers G04D3/00)
Turning-machines in which the workpiece is rotated by means at a distance from the headstock
Turning-machines or devices characterised only by the special arrangement of constructional units (B23Q37/00 takes precedence; such features of general applicability B23Q)
Turning-machines characterised by the use of faceplates
Turret lathes for turning individually-chucked workpieces {(turrets B23B29/24)}
Turning-machines or devices with rotary tool heads {(B23B5/08, B23B5/14 and B23B5/16 take precedence)}
Turning-machines with two or more working-spindles, e.g. in fixed arrangement
Short turning-machines with one or multiple working-spindles attended from the end (B23B3/12 takes precedence)
Associations of only turning-machines directed to a particular metal-working result (if the metal-working result is not essential B23Q39/00)
Turning-machines or devices specially adapted for particular work; Accessories specially adapted therefor
for turning hubs or brake drums (B23B5/04 takes precedence)
for reconditioning hubs or brake drums or axle spindles without removing same from the vehicle
for turning valves or valve bodies {(turning conical surfaces in general B23B5/38; tools for working valve seats B23B51/106)}
for turning axles, bars, rods, tubes, rolls, i.e. shaft-turning lathes, roll lathes; Centreless turning
Cutting-off lathes ({B23D21/00 takes precedence} shearing B23D)
for bevelling, chamfering, or deburring the ends of bars or tubes
for turning crankshafts, eccentrics, or cams, e.g. crankpin lathes
for simultaneously turning internal and external surfaces of a body
for turning wheels or wheel sets or cranks thereon, i.e. wheel lathes
for turning specially-shaped surfaces by making use of relative movement of the tool and work produced by geometrical mechanisms, i.e. forming-lathes

Automatic or semi-automatic turning-machines with a single working-spindle, e.g. controlled by cams; Equipment therefor; Features common to automatic and semi-automatic turning-machines with one or more working-spindles {(arrangements or accessories for enabling machine tools not specially designed only for thread cutting to be used for this purpose B23G3/00)}
Automatic or semi-automatic machines for turning of stock
Automatic or semi-automatic machines for turning of workpieces

Automatic or semi-automatic turning-machines with a plurality of working-spindles, e.g. automatic multiple-spindle machines with spindles arranged in a drum carrier able to be moved into predetermined positions; Equipment therefor (equipment applicable to single-spindle machines B23B7/00)
{Spindle carriers: constructional details, drives for the spindles, or the like}
Automatic or semi-automatic machines for turning of stock
Automatic or semi-automatic machines for turning of workpieces

Automatic or semi-automatic turning-machines incorporating equipment for performing other working procedures, e.g. slotting, milling, rolling {(B23B3/065 and B23B3/16 take precedence; machines incorporating a plurality of sub- assemblies, each capable of performing a metal-working operation, the sub-assemblies being arranged to operate simultaneously at different stations B23Q39/04)}
Arrangements for automatically conveying or chucking or guiding stock
for turning-machines with a single working-spindle
for turning-machines with a plurality of working-spindles
Arrangements for switching-off the drive of turning-machines after the stock has been completely machined
Arrangements for reducing vibrations in feeding-passages or for damping noise (damping noise in general G10K)
Accessories or auxiliary equipment for turning-machines (for machine tools in general B23Q; cooling or lubricating B23Q11/12)
Arrangements for chip-breaking in turning-machines (on cutting tools B23B27/22)
Safety guards specially designed for turning machines ({B23Q11/08 takes precedence;} in general F16P)
Measuring, gauging, or adjusting equipment on turning-machines for setting-on, feeding, controlling, or monitoring the cutting tools or work (measuring devices or gauges G01B)
Tools for turning or boring machines (for drilling machines B23B51/00); Tools of a similar kind in general; Accessories therefor
{Geometry of the chip-forming or the clearance planes, e.g. tool angles (B23B27/141 and B23B27/22 take precedence)}
{for internal turning (boring bars B23B29/02, boring heads B23B29/03; milling cutters B23C5/00; reamers B23D77/00)}
Cutting tools with straight main part and cutting edge at an angle (B23B27/04 - B23B27/08 take precedence)
Cutting-off tools (B23B27/08 takes precedence {; toolholders for cutting-off inserts B23B29/043})
Cutting tools with blade- or disc-like main parts {(with disc-like main parts B23B27/083)}

Cutting tools with special provision for cooling {(drills with lubricating or cooling equipment B23B51/06; features relating to lubricating or cooling of milling cutters B23C5/28; arrangements or devices for cooling or lubricating tools or work B23Q11/10)}
Cutting tools of which the bits or tips {or cutting inserts} are of special material
Cutting tools with chip-breaking equipment {(B23B27/045, B23B27/143, B23B27/16 take precedence; arrangements for chip-breaking B23B25/02; for milling tools B23C5/165)}
Holders for non-rotary cutting tools (B23B27/12 takes precedence); Boring bars or boring heads; Accessories for tool holders
Tool holders for a single cutting tool
Tool holders for a plurality of cutting tools, e.g. turrets {(indexing devices B23Q16/00)}

Chucks {(allowing axial oscillation of percussion tool bits B25D17/08)}; Expansion mandrels; Adaptations thereof for remote control (faceplates B23Q1/50; rotary devices holding by magnetic and/or electrical force acting directly on work B23Q3/152)
{Protection against entering of chips or dust}
Drivers; Driving centres, Nose clutches, e.g. lathe dogs
{Drivers with driving pins or the like}
Methods for boring or drilling, or for working essentially requiring the use of boring or drilling machines; Use of auxiliary equipment in connection with such methods
Boring by making use of vibrations of ultrasonic frequency (working materials by subjecting the grinding tools or the abrading medium to vibration, e.g. grinding with ultrasonic frequency, B24B1/04)
General-purpose boring or drilling machines or devices; Sets of boring and/or drilling machines
Boring machines; Combined horizontal boring and milling machines
Co-ordinate boring or drilling machines; Machines for making holes without previous marking
characterised by the drive, e.g. by fluid-pressure drive pneumatic power drive
with special provision to enable the machine or the drilling or boring head to be moved into any desired position, e.g. with respect to immovable work
Drilling machines with a plurality of working-spindles; Drilling automatons
in which the working position of tool or work is controlled by copying discrete points of a pattern (features of copying devices B23Q35/02)
Associations of only boring or drilling machines directed to a particular metal-working result (if not producing a particular metal-working result B23Q39/00)
Boring or drilling machines or devices specially adapted for particular work {(surgical drilling machines A61B17/16)}; Accessories specially adapted therefor
for boring deep holes; Trepanning, e.g. of gun or rifle barrels
for boring polygonal or other non-circular holes
for boring holes in steam boilers
for forming working surfaces of cylinders, of bearings, e.g. in heads of driving rods, or of other engine parts
for boring holes with high-quality surface
Boring or drilling devices able to be attached to a machine tool, whether or not replacing an operative portion of the machine tool (if specially adapted for particular work B23B41/00)
to the tailstock of a lathe

Hand-held or like portable drilling machines, e.g. drill guns; Equipment therefor (details or components, e.g. casings, bodies, of portable power-driven tools not particularly related to the operation performed B25F5/00)
{Gear boxes, clutches, bearings, feeding mechanisms or like equipment}
driven by fluid-pressure or pneumatic power
Constructional features of components specially designed for boring or drilling machines; Accessories therefor (working-spindles, bearing sleeves therefor B23Q1/70; for machine tools in general B23Q)
Liftable or lowerable drill heads or headstocks; Balancing arrangements therefor {(weight and flexion compensation B23Q11/001)}
Drill jigs for workpieces (equipment for setting or guiding the drill B23B49/00)
Additional gear with one or more working-spindles attachable to the main working-spindle and mounting the additional gear {(multi-spindle drilling machines B23B39/16)}
Arrangements for preventing the running-out of drills or fracture of drills when getting through
Arrangements for removing chips out of the holes made; Chip- breaking arrangements attached to the tool {(chip-breaking in turning machines B23B25/02; in turning tools B23B27/22)}

Measuring or gauging equipment on boring machines for positioning or guiding the drill; Devices for indicating failure of drills during boring; Centering devices for holes to be bored (marking-out equipment B25H7/00; measuring devices, gauges G01B)
{Devices for detecting or indicating failure of drills}
{Stops attached to drilling tools, tool holders or drilling machines (B23B51/104 takes precedence)}
Devices for boring or drilling centre holes in workpieces
Devices for drilling holes in brake bands or brake linings

{Drills with connected cutting heads, e.g. with non-exchangeable cutting heads; Drills with a single insert extending across the rotational axis and having at least two radially extending cutting edges in the working position}
Drills with lubricating or cooling equipment {(B23B51/042 takes precedence)}
Drills combined with tool parts or tools for performing additional working {(B23G5/20 takes precedence)}
Adapters for drills or chucks; Tapered sleeves
Top or side views of the cutting edge
Cross section of the cutting edge
Other features of cutting inserts not covered by B23B2200/04 - B23B2200/32
Fixation of cutting inserts in holders
Fixation using an elastically deformable clamping member
Fixation screws, bolts or pins of particular form
using two or more fixation screws
Systems for indexing the cutting insert automatically
Systems for changing the cutting insert automatically
Tool holders having multiple cutting inserts
Tools comprising weakened spot on the tool at a preferred breakage location
Components of internal combustion engines (B23B2215/16 and B23B2215/20 take precedence)
Details of turning, boring or drilling processes
Turning, boring or drilling at high speeds
Materials of tools or workpieces composed of metals, alloys or metal matrices
Details of hard metal, i.e. cemented carbide
Metal matrices with non-metallic particles or fibres
Materials of tools or workpieces composed of a compound including a metal
Materials of tools or workpieces not comprising a metal
Plastics not otherwise provided for, e.g. nylon
Properties of materials of tools or workpieces, materials of tools or workpieces applied in a specific manner
Cast, i.e. In the form of a casting
Materials having grain size less than 1 micrometre, e.g. nanocrystalline
Details of boring bars or boring heads
Cutting edges of different lengths or at different axial positions
Cutting inserts located on different radii
Boring, facing or grooving heads with integral electric motor
Details of chucks, toolholder shanks or tool shanks
Features of shanks of tools not relating to the operation performed by the tool
Chucks for handtools having means for opening and closing the jaws using the driving motor of the handtool
Chucks for shanks of tools having means for reducing the bending of the retained shanks
Chucks having means to amplify the force produced by the actuating means to increase the clamping force
Chucks with clamping force limitation means
Compensating chucks, i.e. with means for the compensation of irregularities of form or position
Chucks having a pivotal retention element in the form of a laterally acting cam
Chucks operated by a motor which is movable to engage with, or disengage from, the chuck operating means
Devices to counteract clamping forces exerted within the spindle in order to release the tool or workpiece
Chucks with means to loosely retain the tool or workpiece in the unclamped position
Chucks with more than one set of gripping means
Self-grasping, i.e., automatic grasping upon insertion of tool or workpiece
Means to allow the facing of the axial end of the workpiece near the axis of rotation
Centres or drivers comprising a ball
Centres or drivers with a special arrangement of bearings or with special bearings
Centres or drivers with convex surfaces
Centres or drivers supporting the workpiece at three points around the circumference
Turning of brake discs, drums or hubs
Details of connections of tools or workpieces
Connections using hollow screws, e.g. for the transmission of coolant
Shrink-fitted connections, i.e. using heating and cooling to produce interference fits
Connections using a tongue and a hollow of corresponding prismatic form
Jigs using one or more holes as datums for drilling further holes
Jigs for drilling holes for lock sets for doors
Jigs for drilling overlapping or interfering holes
Drilling jigs with means to affix the jig to the workpiece
Jigs for drilling stairs and associated components, e.g. banisters or handrails
Jigs for drilling holes for lock wires in bolts or nuts
Compensating adverse effects during turning, boring or drilling
Surface of tool modified by roughening, scratching, etc. to modify friction or other adverse effect
Details of tools for drilling machines
Connections between shanks and removable cutting heads
Side or plan views of cutting edges
Cross sectional views of the cutting edges
Configuration of the cutting part, i.e. the main cutting edges
New cutting edge by fracture, wear, or recycling
Margins, i.e. the narrow portion of the land which is not cut away to provide clearance on the circumferential surface
Drills having a centre free from cutting edges or with recessed cutting edges
Drills with means for feeding cable
Drilling tools having means to reinforce the shank, e.g. drills having small shanks being gripped by devices having a larger shank
Drills operating in the reverse direction, i.e. in the unscrewing direction of a right-hand thread
Drills with provision to be used as a screwdriver
Drills for drilling a flat bottomed hole
Centre drills of known configuration, e.g. the provision of a centre drill in centres or chucks
Collets of known configuration, i.e. devices using a collet
Devices to regulate the depth of cut
Harmonic drive gearboxes, i.e. reduction gearing including wave generator, flex spline and a circular spline
Hand tools used to operate chucks or to assemble, adjust or disassemble tools or equipment used for turning, boring or drilling
Markings, i.e. symbols or other indicating marks
Suction pads or vacuum cups, e.g. for attachment of guides to workpieces
Details of turning, boring or drilling machines, processes or tools not otherwise provided for
Use of a particular power source
Clamping mechanisms; Provisions for clamping (B23B2210/00 takes precedence)
Centering of two components relative to one another
Constructions comprising exactly two similar components
Constructions comprising three or more similar components
Internally located features, machining or gripping of internal surfaces
Externally located features, machining or gripping of external surfaces
Tool, chuck or other device activated by the coolant or lubrication system of the machine tool
Identification of tooling or other equipment
Methods of turning, boring or drilling not otherwise provided for
Turning, boring or drilling tools or machines with provision for milling
